title: artocarpus lanceifolius subsp. clementis
intro: Artocarpus lanceifolius subsp. clementis, also known as the Polynesian Breadfruit, is a subspecies of the Artocarpus lanceifolius tree, native to the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be used to treat various health issues such as fever, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support digestive health, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. The herbal preparations of Artocarpus lanceifolius subsp. clementis include teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be made by boiling the leaves, bark, or roots in water to harness its medicinal properties.
